_meta.purpose: Page template for a landing page: which section patterns are required / optional / forbidden, and the order rules. Compose a page by SELECTING sections per content (see sections.json → whenToUse) and ordering them per this template — NOT by copying examples.json. examples.json is only a density/style reference.

hero.note: Exactly one hero, always the first section after the Header.

body.note: Choose 2–5 body sections by mapping each content group to the best-fit pattern via sections.json → whenToUse. Do NOT reuse the same pattern for every group when the content types differ.

cta.note: Exactly one closing CTA, always the last section.

rules: Header is always the first child (logo = home; no 'Startseite' nav item).
Zebra backgrounds: hero on color.background.canvas, then alternate surface/canvas across sections.
One consistent centered contentWidth for the whole page.
Action hierarchy: the hero owns the page's primary action (Brand, may add a Filled secondary); the closing CTA is Brand; body sections use Links, never a Brand button per card.
Avoid a mono-layout: if the body has heterogeneous content (e.g. a benefit story AND a set of options), use different patterns for them (e.g. media-text-rows + card-grid), not the same grid twice.
Every body section MUST carry a title (h2); inner headings are h3/h4.
